subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Runs a flow

options.description: Specify the target org. By default, runs against the current default org
If set, deletes the scratch org after the flow completes
Drops into pdb, the Python debugger, on an exception
Pass task specific options for the task as '-o taskname__option value'. You can specify more than one option by using -o more than once
Disables all prompts. Set for non-interactive mode use such as calling from scripts or CI systems
Help for flow run

args.name: FLOW_NAME
args.description: Name of the flow to run

subcommands.name: org
subcommands.description: Commands for connecting and interacting with Salesforce orgs

subcommands.name: browser
subcommands.description: Opens a browser window and logs into the org using the stored OAuth credentials

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. By default, runs against the current default org
Navigate to the specified page after logging in
Display the target URL, but don't open a browser
Help for org browser

subcommands.name: connect
subcommands.description: Connects a new org's credentials using OAuth Web Flow

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. One of ORGNAME (see above) or --org is required
Name of the connected_app service to use
If set, connects to a Salesforce sandbox org
If set, login to this hostname
If set, sets the connected org as the new default org
If set, the connected org is available to all CumulusCI projects
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: default
subcommands.description: Sets an org as the default org for tasks and flows

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. By default, runs against the current default org
Unset the org as the default org leaving no default org selected
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import an org from Salesforce DX

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. One of ORGNAME (see above) or --org is required
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: Display information for a connected org

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. By default, runs against the current default org
Print as JSON. Includes access token
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all orgs in scope for the current project

options.description: Print the table using plain ascii
Output results in JSON format
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: prune
subcommands.description: Removes all expired scratch orgs from the current project

options.description: Remove all scratch orgs, regardless of expiry
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: Removes an org from the keychain

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. One of ORGNAME (see above) or --org is required
Set this option to force remove a global org. Default behavior is to error if you attempt to delete a global org
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: scratch
subcommands.description: Connects a Salesforce DX Scratch Org to the keychain

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. One of ORGNAME (see above) or --org is required
If set, sets the connected org as the new default org
If provided, overrides the devhub used to create the scratch org
If provided, overrides the scratch config default days value for how many days the scratch org should persist
If set, don't set a password for the org
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: scratch_delete
subcommands.description: Deletes a Salesforce DX Scratch Org leaving the config in the keychain for regeneration

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. One of ORGNAME (see above) or --org is required
Show this message and exit

subcommands.name: shell
subcommands.description: Drop into a Python shell with a simple_salesforce connection in `sf`, as well as the `org_config` and `project_config`

options.description: Alternate way to specify the target org. By default, runs against the current default org
Path to a script to run
Python code to run directly
Show this message and exit
